title: polystichum viscidulum
intro: Polystichum viscidulum, also known as the sticky fir fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Polystichum viscidulum include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
